items.text:
Subject: Your Writing Center Appointment Is Confirmed

Dear Marcus,

This email confirms your tutoring appointment for Thursday, October 9, from 3:00 PM to 4:00 PM in Room 215 of Bellamy Hall. Your tutor, Ms. Patel, will help you revise the argument paper for your History 200 course.

Please bring a printed copy of your draft and your assignment sheet. There's no charge for the session itself, but printing in the center costs $0.10 per page, so we'd suggest printing your draft beforehand. If you can't make it, cancel through the student portal at least 24 hours ahead; otherwise a $5 missed-session fee will be added to your account.

Arrive a few minutes early to sign in at the front desk.

Thanks,
The Bellamy Writing Center

format_metadata.subject: Your Writing Center Appointment Is Confirmed

questions.stem: According to the email, what should Marcus bring to his appointment?

options.A: A printed copy of his draft and assignment sheet
options.B: A receipt showing the session fee was paid
options.C: A confirmation code from the student portal
options.D: A reading list provided by his History tutor

questions.explanation: The email instructs Marcus to bring a printed copy of his draft and his assignment sheet. The other options reuse terms like the portal, the fee, and the tutor but attach them to items the email never asks him to bring.

questions.stem: According to the email, how can Marcus avoid the $5 missed-session fee?

options.A: By printing his draft before the session begins
options.B: By canceling through the portal at least 24 hours ahead
options.C: By arriving a few minutes early to sign in
options.D: By emailing Ms. Patel directly about the conflict

questions.explanation: The email states that canceling through the student portal at least 24 hours ahead avoids the $5 missed-session fee. Printing early and arriving early are separate instructions, and contacting the tutor directly is never mentioned.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about printing a draft at the Writing Center?

options.A: It is included free with every tutoring session
options.B: It is required before any appointment can begin
options.C: It is handled entirely by the front desk staff
options.D: It can be avoided by preparing pages in advance

questions.explanation: Because printing costs $0.10 per page and the email suggests printing the draft beforehand, students can avoid that cost by preparing pages in advance. The session is free, but printing is not, and the email never says staff print for students or that printing is required.

items.text:
Notice: Change to Student Package Delivery Procedure

Effective Monday, November 3, all student packages will be processed at the new Mail Center in Whitford Hall, Room 8, rather than at the residence hall front desks. The Mail Center is open Monday through Friday, 10:00 AM to 6:00 PM.

Please review the updated procedure:

- A pickup notice will be sent to your campus email when a package arrives.
- Packages must be collected within five days, or a $3.00 daily storage fee applies.
- A valid student ID is required at the counter; deliveries cannot be released without one.
- Perishable items are not accepted and will be returned to the sender.

Weekend deliveries are now held until the following Monday. Questions should be directed to the Mail Center at mail@campus.edu or 555-0148.

format_metadata.title: Change to Student Package Delivery Procedure

questions.stem: According to the notice, where will student packages now be processed?

options.A: At the residence hall front desks as before
options.B: At a temporary counter near the campus portal
options.C: At the new Mail Center in Whitford Hall
options.D: At the sender's nearest local post office

questions.explanation: The notice states that packages will be processed at the new Mail Center in Whitford Hall, Room 8, rather than at the residence hall front desks. The front-desk option describes the old procedure, and the other locations are not mentioned.

questions.stem: According to the notice, what is required to collect a package at the counter?

options.A: A printed copy of the pickup notice email
options.B: A payment of the $3.00 daily storage fee
options.D: A signature from the residence hall staff

questions.explanation: The notice states that a valid student ID is required at the counter and that deliveries cannot be released without one. The storage fee applies only after five days, and the email notice and a staff signature are not collection requirements.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about a package that arrives on a Saturday?

options.A: It is delivered directly to the student's room
options.B: It will not be available for pickup until Monday
options.C: It is returned to the sender after the weekend
options.D: It is charged the storage fee starting that day

questions.explanation: Since the Mail Center is closed on weekends and weekend deliveries are held until the following Monday, a Saturday package cannot be picked up until Monday. Returns apply only to perishables, and storage fees begin after five days, not on arrival.
